description: Transform Free_Guy into a drop-in simulator game platform. Users register accounts, design characters, drop them into simulation worlds, and observe them living autonomously. Includes JWT auth, character ownership, map selection, WebSocket live observation, and per-simulation access control.

userStories.title: Install and configure JWT authentication
userStories.description: As a developer, I need JWT auth configured in Django so all subsequent auth endpoints can issue and validate tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `djangorestframework-simplejwt` to `frontend_server/requirements.txt`
Add `rest_framework_simplejwt` to INSTALLED_APPS in `frontend_server/frontend_server/settings/base.py`
Set `DEFAULT_AUTHENTICATION_CLASSES` in DRF settings to `[JWTAuthentication]`
Configure SimpleJWT: ACCESS_TOKEN_LIFETIME=15 minutes, REFRESH_TOKEN_LIFETIME=30 days
Add `ROTATE_REFRESH_TOKENS=True` and `BLACKLIST_AFTER_ROTATION=True` to SIMPLE_JWT settings
Add `rest_framework_simplejwt.token_blacklist` to INSTALLED_APPS and run migration
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Map model to database
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Map model so available simulation worlds are stored in the database with no code changes required to add new maps.
acceptanceCriteria: `Map` model added to `frontend_server/translator/models.py` with fields: id (CharField primary key, e.g. 'the_ville'), name (CharField), description (TextField), preview_image_url (CharField, blank=True), maze_name (CharField), max_agents (IntegerField default 25), is_active (BooleanField default True)
Migration generated and applies cleanly against PostgreSQL
Model registered in Django admin with list_display=[id, name, maze_name, max_agents, is_active]
A data migration (or fixture) creates the initial 'the_ville' map entry
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add owner and visibility fields to Simulation model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Simulation to track its owner and visibility so access control can be enforced.
acceptanceCriteria: `Simulation` model gains `owner` ForeignKey to `django.contrib.auth.User` (nullable=True, on_delete=SET_NULL) to preserve existing simulations
`Simulation` model gains `map_id` ForeignKey to `Map` (nullable=True, on_delete=SET_NULL)
`Simulation` model gains `visibility` CharField with choices ['private', 'public', 'shared'], default 'private'
Migration generated and applies cleanly
Admin updated to show owner, map_id, visibility in list_display
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Character model to database
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Character model so users can create and own reusable agent personas.
acceptanceCriteria: `Character` model added to `frontend_server/translator/models.py` with fields: id (auto), owner (FK to User, on_delete=CASCADE), name (CharField), age (IntegerField, nullable), traits (TextField, blank=True), backstory (TextField, blank=True), currently (TextField, blank=True), lifestyle (TextField, blank=True), daily_plan (TextField, blank=True), status (CharField choices=['available','in_simulation'], default='available'), simulation (FK to Simulation, nullable=True, on_delete=SET_NULL)
UNIQUE constraint on (owner, name)
Migration generated and applies cleanly
Model registered in Django admin
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add SimulationMembership model to database
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a SimulationMembership model so per-simulation roles and invite status can be tracked.
acceptanceCriteria: `SimulationMembership` model added with fields: id (auto), simulation (FK to Simulation, on_delete=CASCADE), user (FK to User, on_delete=CASCADE), role (CharField choices=['admin','observer']), status (CharField choices=['invited','active','declined']), invited_at (DateTimeField auto_now_add), joined_at (DateTimeField nullable)
UNIQUE constraint on (simulation, user)
Migration generated and applies cleanly
Model registered in Django admin with list_display=[simulation, user, role, status]
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: User registration API endpoint
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to register an account via POST /api/v1/auth/register/ so I can own characters and simulations.
acceptanceCriteria: `POST /api/v1/auth/register/` accepts `{ username, email, password, password_confirm }`
Returns 400 with field errors if: username/email already taken, passwords don't match, password < 8 chars or all-numeric
On success creates User, returns `{ access, refresh, user: { id, username, email } }` with HTTP 201
Endpoint added to `frontend_server/frontend_server/urls.py`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: User login and token refresh API endpoints
userStories.description: As a registered user, I want to log in and get JWT tokens so I can access protected endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: `POST /api/v1/auth/login/` accepts `{ username, password }`, returns `{ access, refresh, user: { id, username, email } }` on success, 401 on failure
`POST /api/v1/auth/token/refresh/` accepts `{ refresh }`, returns `{ access }` or 401 if refresh token invalid/expired
`POST /api/v1/auth/logout/` accepts `{ refresh }`, blacklists the refresh token, returns 200
All three endpoints added to `frontend_server/frontend_server/urls.py`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: User profile and password reset API endpoints
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want to view/update my profile and reset my password so I can manage my account.
acceptanceCriteria: `GET /api/v1/auth/me/` returns `{ id, username, email, date_joined }` for authenticated user, 401 if unauthenticated
`PATCH /api/v1/auth/me/` accepts `{ username?, email? }`, updates and returns updated user object
`POST /api/v1/auth/password-reset/` accepts `{ email }`, logs reset link to console in development (no real email required), returns 200
`POST /api/v1/auth/password-reset/confirm/` accepts `{ token, password, password_confirm }`, returns 400 if token invalid/expired or passwords don't match, 200 on success
All endpoints added to `frontend_server/frontend_server/urls.py`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Maps list API endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a maps endpoint so the frontend can show available simulation worlds.
acceptanceCriteria: `GET /api/v1/maps/` returns `{ maps: [{ id, name, description, preview_image_url, max_agents }] }` for all active maps
Endpoint requires authentication (401 if unauthenticated)
Added to `frontend_server/frontend_server/urls.py`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update simulation create API to accept map and visibility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the simulation create endpoint to accept map_id, visibility, and set the authenticated user as owner/admin.
acceptanceCriteria: `POST /api/v1/simulations/` now accepts `{ name, map_id?, visibility? }` in addition to existing fields
If `map_id` provided, validates it exists in Map table; returns 400 if not found
`map_id` defaults to `'the_ville'` if omitted
`visibility` defaults to `'private'` if omitted
Creating user is set as `simulation.owner`
A `SimulationMembership` row is created for the creating user with role=`'admin'`, status=`'active'`
Endpoint now requires authentication; returns 401 if unauthenticated
API response includes `map_id` and `visibility` fields
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Character CRUD API endpoints
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want API endpoints to create, list, edit, and delete my characters.
acceptanceCriteria: `GET /api/v1/characters/` returns `{ characters: [...] }` for authenticated user's characters only
`POST /api/v1/characters/` accepts `{ name, age?, traits?, backstory?, currently?, lifestyle?, daily_plan? }`, creates character for authenticated user, returns 201
Returns 400 if name is blank or if (owner, name) combination already exists
`GET /api/v1/characters/{id}/` returns character detail; 404 if not owned by user
`PATCH /api/v1/characters/{id}/` updates character; 403 if not owner; 400 if character is in_simulation
`DELETE /api/v1/characters/{id}/` deletes character; 403 if not owner; 400 with error message if character status is 'in_simulation'
All endpoints require authentication; return 401 if unauthenticated
Added to `frontend_server/frontend_server/urls.py`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Drop character into simulation API endpoint
userStories.description: As a simulation admin, I want to assign a character to a simulation via POST /api/v1/simulations/{id}/drop/ so the AI engine can bring them to life.
acceptanceCriteria: `POST /api/v1/simulations/{sim_id}/drop/` accepts `{ character_id }`
Returns 403 if authenticated user is not the simulation admin (role='admin' in SimulationMembership)
Returns 400 if character does not belong to the authenticated user
Returns 400 if character status is already 'in_simulation'
Returns 404 if character_id not found
On success: sets `character.status='in_simulation'`, sets `character.simulation=sim`, creates Persona row from character fields, returns updated simulation object
Endpoint requires authentication
Added to urls.py
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Start, pause, and resume simulation API endpoints
userStories.description: As a simulation admin, I want to start, pause, and resume my simulation via API so I can control when the engine runs.
acceptanceCriteria: `POST /api/v1/simulations/{sim_id}/start/` sets simulation status to 'running', sets start_date if null; returns 403 if not admin; returns 400 if no characters dropped; returns 409 if already running
`POST /api/v1/simulations/{sim_id}/pause/` sets status to 'paused'; returns 403 if not admin; returns 409 if not running
`POST /api/v1/simulations/{sim_id}/resume/` sets status back to 'running'; returns 403 if not admin; returns 409 if not paused
All three endpoints require authentication
Added to urls.py
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Simulation access control and visibility API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need access control enforced on simulation read endpoints and a PATCH endpoint to update visibility.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `IsSimulationAdmin` DRF permission class: passes if user has SimulationMembership role='admin' for the simulation
Create `CanObserveSimulation` DRF permission class: passes if simulation.visibility='public' OR user has active SimulationMembership for the simulation
`GET /api/v1/simulations/{sim_id}/`, agents, state endpoints now require `CanObserveSimulation`; return 403 if user lacks access
`PATCH /api/v1/simulations/{sim_id}/` accepts `{ visibility? }` (admin only); returns 403 if not admin
`GET /api/v1/simulations/mine/` returns simulations where user has an active SimulationMembership
`GET /api/v1/simulations/public/` returns all simulations with visibility='public' (requires auth)
Added to urls.py
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Simulation invite system API endpoints
userStories.description: As a simulation admin, I want to invite users to observe my simulation, and as an invitee I want to accept or decline.
acceptanceCriteria: `POST /api/v1/simulations/{sim_id}/members/` accepts `{ username }`, creates SimulationMembership with role='observer', status='invited'; returns 403 if not admin; returns 404 if username not found; returns 409 if already a member
`GET /api/v1/simulations/{sim_id}/members/` returns list of members with id, username, role, status; requires CanObserveSimulation
`DELETE /api/v1/simulations/{sim_id}/members/{user_id}/` removes membership; returns 403 if not admin
`GET /api/v1/invites/` returns pending invites (status='invited') for authenticated user
`POST /api/v1/invites/{membership_id}/accept/` sets status='active', sets joined_at=now()
`POST /api/v1/invites/{membership_id}/decline/` sets status='declined'
Both accept/decline return 404 if membership not found or doesn't belong to authenticated user
Added to urls.py
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Redis and Django Channels to docker-compose and requirements
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Redis and Django Channels configured so WebSocket connections can be handled.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `redis:7-alpine` service to `docker-compose.yml` with healthcheck `redis-cli ping`
Add `channels`, `channels-redis`, and `daphne` to `frontend_server/requirements.txt`
Add `channels` and `daphne` to `INSTALLED_APPS` in `frontend_server/frontend_server/settings/base.py`
Configure `CHANNEL_LAYERS` in settings with `RedisChannelLayer` pointing to `redis://redis:6379`
Set `ASGI_APPLICATION = 'frontend_server.asgi.application'`
Create `frontend_server/frontend_server/asgi.py` with Django Channels `ProtocolTypeRouter` routing HTTP to Django and WebSocket to `SimulationConsumer` (file can stub the consumer import for now)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement SimulationConsumer WebSocket handler
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a WebSocket consumer so clients can connect to receive live simulation updates.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `frontend_server/translator/consumers.py` with `SimulationConsumer(AsyncJsonWebsocketConsumer)`

On connect: extract `token` from query string; validate JWT; look up simulation by `sim_id` URL kwarg; enforce CanObserveSimulation logic; reject with close code 4001 if invalid token or 4003 if forbidden
On connect success: add to channel group `simulation_{sim_id}`
On disconnect: remove from channel group
Class method `broadcast_step(sim_id, payload)`: sends payload to group `simulation_{sim_id}` with type `step_update`
WebSocket routing added to `asgi.py`: `path('ws/simulations/<str:sim_id>/', SimulationConsumer.as_asgi())`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend auth state management and API client
userStories.description: As a developer, I need auth state management and an API client in the React frontend so all pages can access the current user and make authenticated requests.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `frontend/src/context/AuthContext.tsx` with `AuthProvider` holding `{ user, accessToken, login, logout, register }` and a `useAuth()` hook
Access token stored in memory (React state); refresh token managed via cookie (sent automatically by browser)
Create `frontend/src/api/client.ts` with a `fetch` wrapper that attaches `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header and auto-calls `/api/v1/auth/token/refresh/` on 401 then retries once
Create `frontend/src/api/auth.ts` with `apiLogin`, `apiRegister`, `apiLogout`, `apiFetchMe` functions
Update `frontend/src/App.tsx` to wrap app in `<AuthProvider>` and add protected route logic: unauthenticated users are redirected to `/login` from protected pages
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Login and registration pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want /login and /register pages so I can create an account and sign in.
acceptanceCriteria: Route `/login` renders a login form with username and password fields and a submit button
Successful login calls `apiLogin`, stores tokens via `AuthContext.login()`, redirects to `/dashboard`
Failed login shows inline error message below the form
Route `/register` renders a registration form with username, email, password, and confirm password fields
Successful registration calls `apiRegister`, auto-logs in, redirects to `/dashboard`
Failed registration shows per-field error messages
Both pages show a link to the other (Login page has 'Create account', Register has 'Already have an account?')
Header nav shows 'Login' / 'Register' links when unauthenticated; shows username + 'Logout' when authenticated
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dashboard page
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want a /dashboard page showing my simulations and characters so I can manage them.
acceptanceCriteria: Route `/dashboard` requires authentication; redirects to `/login` if unauthenticated
Calls `GET /api/v1/simulations/mine/` and displays simulation cards: name, map, status badge (pending/running/paused/completed), character count, step count
Each simulation card has an 'Observe' button linking to `/simulate/{id}` and a 'Settings' button (admin only) linking to `/simulate/{id}/settings`
Shows 'Create simulation' button linking to `/simulations/new`
Below simulations, shows character cards from `GET /api/v1/characters/` with name, status, current simulation (if any)
Shows 'Create character' button linking to `/characters/new`
Empty state messages shown when no simulations or characters exist
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Character creation and list pages
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want /characters and /characters/new pages so I can create and manage my characters.
acceptanceCriteria: Route `/characters` lists all characters from `GET /api/v1/characters/` as cards showing name, age, status
Each card has Edit and Delete buttons; Delete is disabled with tooltip 'Character is in a simulation' when status='in_simulation'
Route `/characters/new` shows a form with fields: Name (required), Age, Traits (who they are), Backstory, Currently (what they're doing), Lifestyle, Daily Plan
All text fields have placeholder examples
On submit calls `POST /api/v1/characters/` and navigates to `/characters` on success
Shows inline error messages on failure
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Simulation creation page with map picker
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want a /simulations/new page with a map picker so I can create a new simulation world.
acceptanceCriteria: Route `/simulations/new` requires authentication
Page fetches `GET /api/v1/maps/` and renders maps as a card grid (2 columns on desktop)
Each map card shows name, description, max_agents hint; selected card has a visible highlight border
'The Ville' is pre-selected by default
Page has a 'Simulation Name' text input (required) and a visibility selector (Private / Public / Shared, defaulting to Private)
Submit button calls `POST /api/v1/simulations/` with name, map_id, and visibility
On success navigates to `/simulate/{sim_id}`
On error shows inline error message
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Simulation detail page with drop character and start controls
userStories.description: As a simulation admin, I want the simulation detail page to let me drop a character and start the simulation.
acceptanceCriteria: Route `/simulate/{sim_id}` fetches simulation metadata and agents list
Admin toolbar visible only to simulation admin (check via `GET /api/v1/simulations/mine/` membership role)
Admin toolbar shows: 'Drop Character' button (opens modal), 'Start' / 'Pause' / 'Resume' button based on current status
'Drop Character' modal shows dropdown of user's available characters (from `GET /api/v1/characters/` filtered to status='available'); submit calls `POST /api/v1/simulations/{id}/drop/`
'Start' button calls `POST /api/v1/simulations/{id}/start/`; becomes 'Pause' after starting
Agents list updates after dropping a character (re-fetches agents)
Shows current simulation status as a badge
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Live WebSocket observation in SimulationViewer
userStories.description: As an observer, I want the simulation viewer to connect via WebSocket and update agent positions in real-time.
acceptanceCriteria: On `/simulate/{sim_id}` mount, open WebSocket to `ws://localhost:8000/ws/simulations/{sim_id}/?token={accessToken}`
On receiving `step_update` message: update agent positions and current simulation time in state
Show green 'Live' indicator when WebSocket is connected; grey 'Disconnected' when not
Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 30s) on unexpected disconnect
On WebSocket close code 4001 (invalid token): attempt token refresh then reconnect once; if still failing redirect to `/login`
On WebSocket close code 4003 (forbidden): show 'Access denied' message, do not reconnect
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Simulation settings page (visibility and members)
userStories.description: As a simulation admin, I want a /simulate/{id}/settings page to manage visibility and invite observers.
acceptanceCriteria: Route `/simulate/{sim_id}/settings` requires admin role; redirects to `/simulate/{sim_id}` if not admin
Shows visibility selector (Private / Shared / Public); changing it calls `PATCH /api/v1/simulations/{sim_id}/` immediately
Shows current members list from `GET /api/v1/simulations/{sim_id}/members/` with name, role, status
Admin can remove any observer member (calls `DELETE /api/v1/simulations/{sim_id}/members/{user_id}/`)
Invite form with username input; submit calls `POST /api/v1/simulations/{sim_id}/members/`; shows success/error feedback
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Pending invites page and header notification badge
userStories.description: As an invited user, I want to see and respond to pending simulation invites.
acceptanceCriteria: Route `/invites` requires authentication; fetches `GET /api/v1/invites/` and lists pending invites
Each invite shows: simulation name, inviting user, Accept and Decline buttons
Accept calls `POST /api/v1/invites/{id}/accept/`; on success simulation appears in dashboard
Decline calls `POST /api/v1/invites/{id}/decline/`; invite disappears from list
App header shows a notification badge with pending invite count (fetches on page load)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Public simulation browser page
userStories.description: As any logged-in user, I want to browse public simulations at /explore so I can discover interesting worlds.
acceptanceCriteria: Route `/explore` requires authentication
Fetches `GET /api/v1/simulations/public/` and shows simulation cards: name, map, status, character count, creator username
Filter bar with 'Status' dropdown (All / Running / Paused / Completed) that appends `?status=` to the API request
'Observe' button on each card navigates to `/simulate/{id}`
Empty state message when no public simulations
Navigation link to `/explore` added to app header
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Simulation replay steps API endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API endpoints for replay data so the frontend can scrub through past simulation steps.
acceptanceCriteria: `GET /api/v1/simulations/{sim_id}/replay/` returns `{ sim_id, total_steps, first_step, last_step }` using MovementRecord table; requires CanObserveSimulation; returns 404 if no steps recorded
`GET /api/v1/simulations/{sim_id}/replay/{step}/` returns agent state at that step from MovementRecord; returns 404 if step not found
Response format for replay step: `{ step, sim_curr_time, agents: { <name>: { x, y, pronunciatio, description } } }`
Added to urls.py
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Replay page with step scrubber
userStories.description: As an observer, I want a /simulate/{id}/replay page with a playback timeline so I can review what my character did.
acceptanceCriteria: Route `/simulate/{sim_id}/replay` fetches `GET /api/v1/simulations/{sim_id}/replay/` to get step range
Renders map canvas with agent positions from current step
Timeline scrubber (range input) spanning from first_step to last_step; dragging updates displayed step
Play button animates through steps at 1 step per second; Pause button stops playback
Speed selector: 1x / 2x / 5x (adjusts playback interval)
Current step number and sim_curr_time displayed above the scrubber
'Watch Live' button shown if simulation is still running; navigates to `/simulate/{sim_id}`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Character detail panel in simulation viewer
userStories.description: As an observer, I want to click an agent on the map and see their current thoughts, plans, and recent memories.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking an agent name or sprite in the simulation viewer fetches `GET /api/v1/simulations/{sim_id}/agents/{agent_id}/`

Opens a side panel showing: agent name, current action description (from scratch.act_description), today's plan list (from scratch.daily_req), last 5 concept nodes (most recent by created date), current conversation partner if any (from scratch.chatting_with)
Panel updates on each WebSocket step_update for the selected agent
Panel closes when clicking outside it or pressing Escape
If no agent is selected the panel is hidden
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Social login with Google and GitHub
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to sign in with Google or GitHub so I don't need a separate password.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `django-allauth`, `dj-rest-auth[with_social]` to `frontend_server/requirements.txt`
Configure allauth in settings: INSTALLED_APPS includes `allauth`, `allauth.account`, `allauth.socialaccount`, `allauth.socialaccount.providers.google`, `allauth.socialaccount.providers.github`
`GET /api/v1/auth/social/google/` initiates Google OAuth flow
`GET /api/v1/auth/social/github/` initiates GitHub OAuth flow
On successful OAuth callback, issues the same JWT pair as regular login
If OAuth email matches an existing account, links social login to that account instead of creating a duplicate
Login page shows 'Continue with Google' and 'Continue with GitHub' buttons below the password form
SOCIALACCOUNT_PROVIDERS configured in settings with placeholder client_id/secret (read from env vars `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, `GITHUB_CLIENT_ID`, `GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET`)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
